meta_title: Roofing in Ramblewood, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofs in Ramblewood, FL. CertainTeed & West Lake systems. Licensed, A+ BBB. Get a free estimate today!
h1: Trusted Roofing Solutions for Ramblewood Homeowners Who Refuse to Compromise
intro:
Ramblewood is one of Broward County's most established residential communities, and the homes here — spread across Ramblewood East and Ramblewood West — reflect the kind of long-term investment that deserves serious protection. With average home values hovering around $340,000 and nearly 45% of the housing stock predating 1994, a significant portion of Ramblewood roofs were built to an earlier generation of standards — standards that simply weren't designed with today's storm science or modern Florida Building Code requirements in mind. For homeowners near Ramblewood Park or tucked along the quiet streets that border Coral Springs and Margate, the roof overhead is the single most critical barrier between a comfortable home and costly water intrusion, structural damage, or a denied insurance claim.

South Florida's climate is relentless. The combination of intense UV exposure, heavy seasonal rains, and the ever-present threat of tropical systems means that even a roof in seemingly decent condition can be quietly failing beneath the surface. Granule loss, lifted flashing, compromised decking, and inadequate underlayment are all issues that compound silently until a storm makes them catastrophic. Ramblewood sits approximately 10 miles inland from the coast, which means it's out of the immediate salt-air corrosion zone — but it absolutely is not out of the path of hurricane-force winds. The 33065 ZIP code falls within a 140 mph wind zone, making proper roofing materials and installation methods more than just a preference — they're a legal and financial necessity.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped more than 500 South Florida homeowners make the transition from outdated, underperforming roofs to hurricane-rated systems that meet or exceed current code. Our crews are 100% in-house — not subcontracted — and bring over 60 years of combined installation experience to every Ramblewood project. Whether you're dealing with storm damage from a recent system, preparing for an insurance inspection, or simply recognizing that your 25-year-old shingle roof has reached the end of its serviceable life, we're here to walk you through every step of the process.

section_why.h2: Why Ramblewood Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Roof Replacement and Repair
section_why.body:
Choosing a roofing contractor in South Florida isn't just about price — it's about trust, accountability, and knowing the crew on your roof actually knows what they're doing. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation as 'The Kings of Service' because we never cut corners on workmanship, and we never hand your project off to a rotating roster of subcontractors. Every roofer on our team is a direct employee of Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, trained to our standards, and supervised by project managers who have spent decades working specifically in Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties.

For Ramblewood homeowners, that local expertise matters in very specific ways. We understand how older homes in Ramblewood East and Ramblewood West were constructed — many with 4/12 or lower-pitch rooflines, limited attic ventilation, and original wood decking that may have absorbed decades of moisture. We assess each job individually, identifying not just what's visible from the street but what's happening at the substrate level. Our inspections cover decking integrity, ventilation adequacy, flashing conditions, and drip edge compliance — all areas that directly affect whether your roof passes a Broward County inspection and qualifies for maximum wind mitigation credits.

Our A+ BBB rating isn't a marketing badge — it reflects how we actually operate. We pull every permit required by the Broward County Building Division before a single shingle is lifted. We communicate openly with homeowners about timelines, material choices, and what to expect during the inspection process. And we don't disappear after the final nail is driven. Our warranty support and post-installation service are part of what sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart in a market crowded with fly-by-night operators who surge into neighborhoods after every hurricane and vanish before the next season begins.

With financing options starting at just $87 per month and $0 down programs available for qualified homeowners, there's no reason to delay a roof that's overdue. A new hurricane-rated roof on a Ramblewood home isn't just a repair — it's a meaningful increase in property value, insurability, and peace of mind for years to come.

section_history.h2: Ramblewood's Storm History Makes a Strong Case for Upgrading Your Roof Now
section_history.body:
Ramblewood has weathered eight named storms since 1990, and three of those left lasting marks on the community's housing stock. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 remains the benchmark storm for South Florida roofing — a catastrophic event that exposed the fragility of pre-code construction and ultimately forced a complete overhaul of the Florida Building Code. Homes in Ramblewood East and Ramblewood West that survived Andrew without full roof replacement often show residual structural vulnerabilities that have never been properly addressed, from compromised roof-to-wall connections to aged sheathing that absorbed wind-driven rain for hours during the storm's passage.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 struck with rapid intensification and delivered widespread wind damage across Broward County, peeling back shingles, dislodging ridge caps, and saturating attics throughout communities just like Ramblewood. Many homeowners patched rather than replaced after Wilma, and those patched roofs are now approaching or exceeding their useful service life. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which moved directly over Broward County and served as a clear reminder that interior communities are not buffered from major storm impacts just because they're miles from the beach.

The cumulative effect of these storms — and the seven others since 1990 — is that Ramblewood's roofing infrastructure is under ongoing stress. For the 45% of homes built before 1994, the risk is compounded by materials and installation methods that predate current hurricane-resistance standards. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing approaches every Ramblewood roof evaluation with this history in mind, treating storm preparedness not as a feature of roofing but as its foundation.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Compliance and Permit Requirements for Ramblewood Roofing Projects
section_code.body:
Ramblewood falls within Broward County's jurisdiction, and all roofing work here is subject to the 2023 Florida Building Code — one of the most rigorous residential construction standards in the United States. While the 33065 ZIP code is not classified as part of the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which applies to Miami-Dade and portions of Broward closer to the coast, Ramblewood homeowners still live in a 140 mph wind design zone. This means every roofing product installed must carry appropriate Miami-Dade or Florida Product Approval ratings, and the installation itself must meet the nailing patterns, underlayment specifications, and fastener requirements defined by current code.

All roofing permits in Ramblewood are pulled through the Broward County Building Division, which requires detailed submittal documentation including product approval numbers, engineer-stamped plans for certain project types, and a licensed contractor of record. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this entire process on your behalf — from permit application through final inspection sign-off. We've developed an efficient working relationship with Broward County inspectors and understand exactly what's required for a clean approval, which saves our clients time and eliminates the risk of failed inspections due to documentation errors or non-compliant materials.

Current code also mandates specific underlayment systems — typically a self-adhering modified bitumen base sheet or approved synthetic underlayment — along with drip edge installation at both eaves and rakes, and enhanced fastening at roof perimeters and corners where wind uplift forces are highest. For homes in Ramblewood East or Ramblewood West that are undergoing full replacement, we also assess the existing decking for compliance with current span and thickness requirements. Any decking that doesn't meet code gets replaced before the new system is installed, ensuring the finished roof is built on a solid, compliant foundation from the ground up.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed and West Lake Roofing Systems Built for Ramblewood's Wind and Weather Demands
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premium roofing systems ideally suited for Ramblewood's climate and code requirements: CertainTeed architectural shingles — specifically the Landmark series — and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product lines are engineered to withstand South Florida's combination of high wind speeds, intense UV exposure, and seasonal moisture, and both carry the product approvals necessary for installation in Broward County's 140 mph wind zone.

The CertainTeed Landmark series is one of the most respected architectural shingle lines in North America. These dimensional shingles feature a dual-layer construction that provides outstanding depth, durability, and wind resistance — rated to withstand winds up to 130 mph with proper installation, and enhanced when installed with our upgraded fastening protocols to meet Broward County's requirements. The Landmark series also benefits from CertainTeed's proprietary Copper Granules Plus algae resistance technology, which is particularly valuable in South Florida's humid subtropical climate where black algae streaking is a common problem on roofs throughout neighborhoods like Ramblewood West. Available in a wide range of colors and profiles, they're a cost-effective option for homeowners who want strong performance without the premium price of tile.

For homeowners seeking the classic Florida aesthetic and maximum long-term durability, West Lake concrete and clay tile systems are an exceptional choice. Tile roofing has been the dominant style across South Florida for generations, and for good reason — properly installed tile can last 40 to 50 years, withstands high wind events with the right attachment systems, and dramatically enhances curb appeal and resale value. West Lake's concrete tile options provide outstanding strength-to-weight performance, while their clay tile line delivers a traditional Mediterranean look that complements the architectural character found throughout Ramblewood East. Both systems are installed by our in-house crews using code-compliant mortar-set or mechanically fastened methods depending on the application.

section_cost.h2: What Ramblewood Homeowners Can Expect to Invest in a New Roof — and How to Finance It
section_cost.body:
For a home in Ramblewood valued around $340,000, a full roof replacement represents one of the highest-return home improvement investments you can make. Depending on the size of the home, roof pitch, material selection, and condition of the existing decking, a complete roof replacement in the 33065 area typically ranges from $12,000 to $22,000 for architectural shingles and $18,000 to $35,000 or more for concrete or clay tile systems. These ranges account for permit costs, decking inspection and replacement where needed, underlayment, and all associated labor performed by our in-house crews.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes this investment accessible through flexible financing options, including $0 down programs and monthly payment plans starting at just $87 per month for qualified homeowners. We work with multiple lending partners to find terms that fit your budget without requiring you to drain savings or delay a project that's genuinely urgent. Roofing is not the kind of home improvement you want to put off — a failing roof compounds its own damage with every rain event, and the cost of interior repairs, mold remediation, and structural remediation can easily dwarf the cost of timely roof replacement.

From a return-on-investment perspective, a new hurricane-rated roof in Ramblewood does several things simultaneously: it protects the structural integrity of a $340,000 asset, it satisfies insurance carriers who are increasingly scrutinizing older roofs in Broward County, and it adds measurable resale value when the home goes to market. Industry data consistently shows that new roofs recoup 60 to 70 percent of their cost in added home value at resale — and in South Florida, where buyers are acutely aware of insurance costs and roof age, a new roof can be the single biggest factor in a smooth, competitive sale.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Ramblewood Roof Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Broward County has become one of the most significant ongoing costs for Ramblewood residents, and the age and condition of your roof is one of the primary factors carriers use to determine your premium — or whether they'll insure you at all. Many major carriers operating in Florida have implemented strict roof age limits, often refusing to write new policies or renew existing ones on homes with roofs older than 15 to 20 years. For the nearly half of Ramblewood homes built before 1994, this is an immediate and urgent concern.

Replacing an aging roof with a compliant, hurricane-rated system opens the door to significant premium savings through Florida's wind mitigation credit program. After a new roof installation, homeowners are eligible for a licensed wind mitigation inspection — a process Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is very familiar with. The inspection evaluates your roof's construction features including roof shape, deck attachment, edge details, and opening protection. Homes that score well across these categories — particularly those with hip roofs and enhanced fastening systems — can see insurance premium reductions of 20 to 40 percent annually. For a Ramblewood homeowner currently paying $4,000 to $6,000 per year for coverage, that's a real and recurring financial benefit.

Carriers like Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and Heritage Insurance are all active in the Broward market and all respond to wind mitigation reports when calculating premiums. CertainTeed Landmark shingles and West Lake tile systems both carry the product approvals that underwriters look for when verifying code-compliant installations. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all necessary documentation after project completion — including the Notice of Commencement, permit card, and product approval references — so your wind mitigation inspector has everything they need to maximize your credit and get your new lower premium in place as quickly as possible.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Ramblewood home's roof needs full replacement versus repairs?
faqs.a: The answer depends on the roof's age, overall condition, and extent of damage. Homes in Ramblewood East and Ramblewood West that were built before 1994 and haven't had a full replacement are almost certainly overdue. Key indicators include granule loss in gutters, curling or cracked shingles, sagging decking, visible daylight in the attic, or repeated leaks after rain events. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers thorough, no-obligation roof inspections to help you make the right call with full information.

faqs.q: What does a new roof typically cost in Ramblewood, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: In the 33065 ZIP code, full roof replacements generally range from $12,000 to $22,000 for architectural shingles and $18,000 to $35,000 for tile systems, depending on home size, pitch, and material selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners. We work with multiple lending partners to find terms that match your budget, and we'll walk you through all options during your free estimate consultation.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Ramblewood, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes — all roofing replacements in Ramblewood require a permit issued through the Broward County Building Division. The permit process involves submitting product approvals, contractor licensing documentation, and project scope details before work begins, followed by a final inspection from a county building official. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf. We submit all required documentation, coordinate the inspection, and ensure your project closes with a final approval — protecting you legally and for future insurance purposes.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a typical Ramblewood home?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements on single-family homes in Ramblewood are completed in one to three days of active installation, depending on the size of the home, the roofing system selected, and decking conditions discovered during tear-off. Tile roofs may take slightly longer due to the weight management and setting processes involved. The permitting and scheduling phase typically adds one to two weeks prior to the start date. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps homeowners informed at every stage and provides a clear project timeline before work begins.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Broward County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs your new hurricane-rated roof and the county issues a final permit, you can commission a licensed wind mitigation inspection. This inspection documents your roof's compliance with current code, including deck attachment, edge detailing, and product approvals. Strong wind mitigation scores routinely result in premium reductions of 20 to 40 percent with carriers active in Broward County. Many Ramblewood homeowners recover a meaningful portion of their roof investment through these annual premium savings over time.

cta_body: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is ready to assess your Ramblewood home's roofing needs at no cost and no obligation — call us today or fill out our quick online form to schedule your free inspection with South Florida's Kings of Service.
